title: The fish rots from the head
author: Systemic Diagnosis
content: “A collapse of output or morale in a system is fundamentally a failure of the leadership/governance layer.”
action: Record: When analyzing system failure, do not look at the bottom-level units; audit the decision-making at the top.

title: Loose lips sink ships
author: Operational Security
content: “In high-stakes environments, the smallest uncontrolled information leak provides the enemy with the vector for destruction.”
action: Record: Treat all internal data as classified. Asymmetry is maintained only through silence.

title: No good deed goes unpunished
author: Game Theory / Incentives
content: “Actions that prioritize external benefit over self-interest often violate the incentive structure of the current system, resulting in immediate friction.”
action: Record: Before acting, model the systemic incentives. If your action disrupts the equilibrium, prepare for immediate retaliation.

title: The devil is in the details
author: Engineering Forensics
content: “Large-scale failure is rarely caused by the obvious architecture; it is triggered by overlooked, minor, or low-level technical specifics.”
action: Record: Perform deep audits on the secondary and tertiary variables; high-level success masks low-level rot.

title: Better the devil you know
author: Risk Management
content: “A known, hostile threat is strategically superior to an unknown variable because its behavior is predictable and actionable.”
action: Record: Prioritize risk-predictability over risk-avoidance. Unknown variables are the primary source of system rupture.
